title: Multi-session swarm visibility query surface (SessionAggregateQueries)
goal: Land the canonical query surface that operators and diagnostics consumers use to inspect multi-session swarm execution: 'all activity in model_session X', 'all sessions touching wp_id Y', 'top 10 slowest spans by activity_kind', 'session timeline from t0 to t1'. These queries close the loop on cluster X observability: spans + FR events + checkpoints + EventLedger joined into one query API.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/observability/aggregate_queries.rs with `SessionAggregateQueries`, query result DTOs, error types; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/observability_aggregate_queries_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-189 MT executor (provides observable workload), MT-197 span repo, MT-198 event registry, MT-199 FR integration all merged.
implementation_notes: Define typed query methods on `SessionAggregateQueries { pool: PgPool }`: (1) `activity_for_model_session(model_session_id, from_utc, to_utc, limit) -> Vec<ActivityRow>` joins kernel_activity_span + kernel_model_session_span + kernel_event_ledger on model_session_id, returns interleaved timeline; (2) `sessions_touching_wp(wp_id, from_utc, to_utc) -> Vec<SessionSummary>` queries kernel_micro_task_job by wp_id, joins through sessions; (3) `slowest_spans_by_activity_kind(activity_kind, limit) -> Vec<SpanLatencyRow>` returns top-N by duration; (4) `session_timeline(session_id, from_utc, to_utc) -> SessionTimeline` produces a chronologically ordered list of events, span boundaries, mailbox messages (via MT-177), checkpoints (via MT-190), MT outcomes (via MT-188); (5) `swarm_concurrency_snapshot(now: SystemTime) -> SwarmSnapshot` returns currently-active sessions count, active leases count, in-flight MTs, pending mailbox messages — for live diagnostics panel rendering. Each method returns a typed DTO (no raw serde_json::Value at the API boundary). All queries paginated (offset + limit with defaults: limit=100, max=1000) to prevent runaway result sets. Performance contract: each query returns within 2s for a workload of 100 sessions x 10k events. Use prepared statements (sqlx::query_as!) where possible. Tests: (a) seed fixture with 3 sessions + spans + events, query each method, assert expected shape; (b) pagination: limit=10 returns exactly 10 rows; (c) latency assertion via timing wrapper (test fails if any query > 5s on the fixture); (d) timeline query produces strict chronological order across mixed entity types.
risk_if_missed: Without aggregate queries, operators must hand-craft SQL to debug multi-session swarms; the diagnostics panel (cluster A AC-DIAG-PANEL-*) has no backend surface for its visualization; the cluster X observability slice fails to deliver operator-actionable visibility.
validator_focus: Every query method has typed DTO return (no raw JSON exposure); pagination enforced (no unbounded result sets); performance contract tested (latency assertion in test suite).
acceptance_criteria_refs: AC-DIAG-PANEL-SURFACES (backend surface for diagnostics panel queries)

implementation_record.summary: MT-200 was upgraded from fixture-only aggregate queries to a dual-backend SessionAggregateQueries surface. SessionAggregateQueries::new(PgPool) now runs typed Postgres SQL joins across kernel_activity_span, kernel_model_session_span, kernel_event_ledger, kernel_session_checkpoint, role_mailbox_message, kernel_micro_task_job, kernel_mt_outcome, and role_mailbox_claim_lease. SessionAggregateQueries::from_fixture remains for fast deterministic tests. Added Offset plus explicit *_page methods so every list/timeline query supports offset+limit pagination while existing methods default to offset 0.
implementation_record.contract_deviation_note: Prior fixture-only deviation is superseded. The contract-owned observability::aggregate_queries path re-exports the folded X.4 span implementation, and the production constructor is now SessionAggregateQueries::new(PgPool). PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT remains only at source-location level: DTOs and implementation live in flight_recorder::spans and are re-exported through observability::aggregate_queries.

red_team_coverage.typed_dtos: observability_aggregate_queries_tests::mt200_queries_return_typed_shapes compiles and asserts typed ActivityRow, SessionSummary, SpanLatencyRow, SessionTimeline, and SwarmSnapshot outputs without raw serde_json::Value at the API boundary.
red_team_coverage.pagination_cap: observability_aggregate_queries_tests::mt200_pagination_limit_caps_rows asserts Limit::new(10) returns exactly 10 rows and Limit::new(10000) caps to 1000.
red_team_coverage.offset_pagination: observability_aggregate_queries_tests::mt200_offset_pagination_returns_stable_windows asserts offset+limit windows for activity rows, sessions, slowest spans, and timelines; the ignored Postgres proof also exercises SQL OFFSET on activity_for_model_session_page.
red_team_coverage.timeline_ordering: observability_aggregate_queries_tests::mt200_session_timeline_is_strictly_chronological_across_entity_types seeds out-of-order event/checkpoint/mailbox entries and asserts chronological order.
red_team_coverage.latency: observability_aggregate_queries_tests::mt200_fixture_queries_return_under_latency_budget seeds 100 sessions and 10000 activity rows, then asserts representative aggregate queries complete under 5 seconds.
red_team_coverage.live_postgres_joins: observability_aggregate_queries_tests::mt200_postgres_queries_join_spans_mailbox_checkpoints_and_events runs against real Postgres and joins the span, event ledger, checkpoint, mailbox, MT job/outcome, and lease tables into typed DTOs.

out_of_scope_self_evaluation.why: The implementation had to add and continue using the folded flight_recorder::spans implementation because MT-196 through MT-199 already colocated X.4 span DTOs and repos there, while the MT owned_files only named observability::aggregate_queries and its tests. The exact follow-up touched flight_recorder::spans for the PgPool backend and offset pagination, and observability::aggregate_queries for contract-path re-export.

postgres_test_url_aggregate_query_rerun_.summary: Live POSTGRES_TEST_URL pass implemented/fixed the residual Postgres paths: MT-189 durable executor smoke, Locus GetMtProgress parity on Postgres, atomic scheduler claim to prevent duplicate SKIP LOCKED claims, checkpoint timestamp precision normalization, and raw_sql schema application for multi-statement Postgres migrations.
product_changes: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs implements LocusOperation::GetMtProgress with the same structured progress metadata projection as SQLite.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs now claims with one atomic UPDATE ... FROM candidate ... RETURNING job_id statement guarded by FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and state=queued.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_loop_control.rs normalizes checkpoint created_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision at creation time.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs and src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs use sqlx::raw_sql for multi-statement schema setup.
src/backend/handshake_core/tests/observability_span_repo_tests.rs uses sqlx::raw_sql for multi-statement migration setup.
verification_caveats: A broad cargo test command filtered by process_ledger::mt_loop_control::tests but not scoped with --lib attempted to compile unrelated integration tests and failed on Windows paging-file exhaustion; the corrected --lib command passed.
Pre-existing warnings remain in unrelated code paths and were not addressed in this session.
Several modified backend files are untracked in this worktree; tracked-file git diff --check was clean for the tracked subset.

note: Span repository live Postgres suite passed.

implementation_or_hardening_notes: Postgres session_messages.token_count now decodes nullable INT8 or INT4, closing the model_session_scheduler token_count ColumnDecode failure exposed by live Postgres.
model_run cloud-consent terminal paths persist the ModelSession state before emitting terminal session events, closing the Completed-job/Active-session split seen on the valid-bundle path.

Spawned workflow finalization errors are no longer discarded silently; if the job is still non-terminal, the node, job, and workflow run are marked failed best-effort with workflow_finalization_failed evidence.
Postgres workflow_node_executions now set finished_at for completed_with_issues and poisoned terminal statuses, with a live Postgres regression.

postgres_test_url_live_evidence_20260525.summary: Post-kbstart live POSTGRES_TEST_URL model_session_scheduler_tests proof. The exact scheduler queue path and full default-threaded test binary both pass inside 20-minute command bounds after Postgres-only harness/schema fixes and session registry/worktree hardening. Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator status remains PENDING.
implementation_or_hardening_notes: model_session_scheduler_tests now use a live Postgres-only harness with isolated schemas and focused schema installation; no SQLite harness path is used.
Focused schema includes ai_job_mcp_fields so session observability spans can persist MCP/tool-call correlation rows.
SessionRegistry keeps worktree allocations through terminal finalization so persisted ModelSession state does not regress to Blocked after completion.
Model-run dispatch locking is per SessionRegistry rather than a process-global async lock, reducing cross-test contention while preserving deterministic dispatch within a registry.
Session worktree add/remove operations are serialized around git worktree metadata.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: SessionAggregateQueries upgraded from fixture-only to dual-backend (PgPool production + fixture for tests). Typed DTOs (ActivityRow, SessionSummary, SpanLatencyRow, SessionTimeline, SwarmSnapshot) — no raw serde_json::Value at API boundary (red_team #1 met via Limit newtype). Offset+limit pagination on every list/timeline. 5/5 fast tests pass + Postgres-gated end-to-end join across kernel_activity_span + kernel_model_session_span + kernel_event_ledger + kernel_session_checkpoint + role_mailbox_message + kernel_micro_task_job + kernel_mt_outcome + role_mailbox_claim_lease. Latency assertion < 5s on 100-session/10k-activity fixture. Implementation lives at flight_recorder/spans.rs with re-export through observability/aggregate_queries.rs (the contract path) — acceptable folded location.
